10. Region of space that contains no matter.
down: 6. Curved path around a celestial body
8. Blank metal block used to fill short lines of type.

hints.down-1: The basin-like opening or mouth of a volcano, through which the chief eruption comes; similarly, the mouth of a geyser, about which a cone of silica is often built up.
hints.across-2: An object or place in which something is stored.
hints.across-3: Each of the seven major bodies which move relative to the fixed stars in the night sky—the Moon, Mercury, Venus, the Sun, Mars, Jupiter and Saturn.
hints.down-4: To smile broadly or especially cheerfully.
hints.across-5: Measured by the progress or revolution of the sun in the ecliptic; as, the solar year.
hints.down-6: An elliptical movement of an object about a celestial object or Lagrange point, especially a periodic elliptical revolution.
hints.down-8: A joke used to fill long days of setting type.
hints.down-9: Central part of a fruit, containing the kernels or seeds.
hints.across-10: Condition of rarefaction, or reduction of pressure below that of the atmosphere, in a vessel, such as the condenser of a steam engine, which is nearly exhausted of air or steam, etc.
hints.down-10: Having no incumbent; unoccupied; said of offices etc.
hints.across-11: In the Solar system, such a body that orbits within the orbit of Jupiter
